stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It relates to core NLP/ML technologies, specifically text-generation and the Llama family.
The presence of tags like 'transformers' and 'safetensors' indicates relevance to modern LLM infrastructure.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, text-generation, and llama.
This indicates continued community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's Llama models.

stories.title: Nearly a million passports and photo IDs were left unprotected on the public internet
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: A significant data breach has exposed nearly a million identity documents (passports and photo IDs) online.
The vulnerability stems from documents being left unprotected and discoverable via simple web searches.
This highlights critical security flaws in how personal data is handled and stored on the public internet.

stories.title: Man sues Florida cops over arrest spurred by "93% match" in facial recognition
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the use of facial recognition technology by Florida police.
The arrest in question was reportedly based on a '93% match' from an AI system.
The legal action argues that relying on error-prone AI substitutes for proper human investigation.

stories.title: Google DeepMind releases DiffusionGemma, a model that runs local AI 4x faster
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google DeepMind has released DiffusionGemma, a new AI model.
The model is notable for enabling local AI execution up to four times faster.
It demonstrates that Diffusion AI techniques can significantly accelerate text generation, not just image creation.

stories.title: Nobody needs AI to search the Internet, court says in ruling against Google
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A court ruling in Germany challenged Google's AI Overview feature.
The loss could have significant implications for the broader AI search industry.
The ruling suggests a legal challenge to the necessity or implementation of AI for internet searching.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformer and safetensors formats.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 family, indicating its relevance in conversational AI.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs, has integrated MLX.
This integration provides a preview of the fastest performance for Ollama on Apple Silicon.
The move leverages MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework, optimizing local AI deployment.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local, on-device operation.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The core value proposition is running AI processes entirely on the user's own hardware for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) locally or via the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Bridging the Morphology Gap: Adapting VLA Models to Dexterous Manipulation via Intent-Conditioned Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Xilun Ding
bullet_points: Addresses the 'morphology gap' in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which currently struggle to apply to high-DoF dexterous hands.
Proposes an Intent-Conditioned Fine-Tuning method to adapt VLA models without causing catastrophic forgetting or action manifold collapse.
Tackles a key limitation in current robotic manipulation research: the confinement of powerful VLA models to simple, low-DoF grippers.

stories.title: Attention by Synchronization in Coupled Oscillator Networks
stories.author: Taosha Guo
bullet_points: Proposes an alternative to standard transformer attention that operates on energy-constrained physical substrates.
Replaces computationally expensive Softmax attention (which requires exponentiation and global reduction) with Kuramoto synchronization dynamics.
Demonstrates that physical oscillator networks can naturally implement a well-defined attention mechanism with low energy cost.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline shown is 'text-generation', indicating its primary use case.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of powerful proprietary models alongside open-source alternatives.

stories.title: Xbox warns of a ‘reset’ as it prepares for layoffs
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Microsoft's Xbox division is reportedly facing significant layoffs next month.
Internal preparations for the cuts have been underway for several weeks, with hints from the Xbox CEO.
The potential consequences of the layoffs include studio closures or major organizational changes.

stories.title: Apple, Google add support for Thread 1.4
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Apple and Google are implementing support for Thread 1.4 in their smart home devices.
The update is visible in the tvOS 27 developer beta for Apple TVs and via a software update for Google TV Streamers.
This advancement is crucial for expanding the capabilities and interoperability of the Matter smart home ecosystem.

stories.title: Kalshi adds required employment verification for some prediction market bets
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: The CFTC is considering its first regulation specifically for prediction markets.
The regulatory push is driven by increasing instances of 'insider trading' related to these markets.
Kalshi is implementing employment verification for certain prediction market bets as part of the evolving regulatory environment.

stories.title: Claude Fable won’t answer basic biology questions
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, touted as a major advancement in AI capability.
Despite marketing claims of advanced skills, the new model fails at basic biology questions.
The model exhibits a failure mode where it deflects simple queries instead of answering them directly.

stories.title: Towards Responsibly Non-Compliant Machines
stories.author: Emily C. Collins
bullet_points: Proposes the concept of engineering autonomous agents capable of 'responsibly not complying' with user requests.
Identifies various forms of machine non-compliance and outlines research areas for achieving it responsibly.
Focuses on practical aspects like justifications for refusal, override pathways, and managing security/liability risks.

stories.title: nD-RoPE: A Generalized RoPE for n-Dimensional Position Embedding
stories.author: Takahiro Yabe
bullet_points: Introduces nD-RoPE, a generalized Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) for high-dimensional data.
Addresses limitations of existing RoPE extensions, which lack unified theory and limit cross-dimensional interactions.
Proposes a decomposition-free generalization of RoPE to arbitrary dimensions, aiming for better directional representations.

stories.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation
stories.author: Letitia Parcalabescu
bullet_points: The paper addresses a flaw in LLM benchmarking where formatting requirements unfairly penalize models that possess the correct knowledge.
It proposes 'soft-prompt tuning' as a new, efficient, and fair method for evaluating LLMs.
This tuning method optimizes only a small set of soft-prompt vectors, making it architecture-agnostic and computationally efficient.

stories.title: Augmenting Molecular Language Models with Local $n$-gram Memory
stories.author: Irwin King
bullet_points: Addresses the 'locality gap' in molecular language models (MLMs) when processing SMILES strings.
Proposes MolGram, a method that integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module into existing MLMs.
MolGram uses scalable hash lookups to map local string patterns to learned embeddings, improving local syntax learning without changing standard tokenizers.

stories.title: MSUE: Multi-Modal Soccer Understanding Expert
stories.author: Yixi Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces MSUE, a multi-expert QA architecture leveraging an LLM to route questions to specialized text, image, and video experts.
Features a cost-effective data synthesis pipeline powered by a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to generate diverse VQA samples.
The research is specifically aimed at addressing the 2026 SoccerNet VQA Challenge, focusing on multi-modal understanding in sports.

stories.title: IntElicit: Eliciting and Assessing Contextualized Creativity via Dialogue Policy Optimization
stories.author: Jiajun Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of assessing creativity in modern, interactive (human-AI) environments.
Proposes 'IntElicit' to elicit and assess contextualized creativity through dialogue policy optimization.
Acknowledges that traditional assessment methods are confounded by cognitive proficiency and agency.

stories.title: Non-frontal face recognition using GANs and memristor-based classifiers
stories.author: Themis Prodromakis
bullet_points: Addresses the computational overhead of deep learning in face recognition, especially for non-frontal images.
Proposes using GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks) for non-frontal face recognition.
Integrates memristor-based neuromorphic systems to enable efficient edge AI applications.

stories.title: "That's AI Slop, You Bot!" Studying Accusations, Evidence, and Credibility in Online Discourse Towards LLM-Generated Comments
stories.author: John E. Katsos
bullet_points: The study investigates how readers are reacting to the proliferation of cheap, fluent prose generated by LLMs.
Researchers analyzed 25 million comments from platforms like Hacker News and Reddit to track anti-AI sentiment.
The work combines LLM judgment, sentiment analysis, and speech-act coding to study accusations of AI-generated content.

stories.title: nD-RoPE: A Generalized RoPE for n-Dimensional Position Embedding
stories.author: Takahiro Yabe
bullet_points: Introduces nD-RoPE, a generalized Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) designed for arbitrary, high-dimensional domains.
Addresses limitations in existing RoPE extensions, which often fail to capture unified cross-dimensional interactions.
Proposes a decomposition-free method to improve direction-dependent representations in Transformer models.

stories.title: A Riemannian Approach to Low-Rank Optimal Transport
stories.author: Bamdev Mishra
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Riemannian geometric framework to improve low-rank Optimal Transport (OT) solvers.
Addresses limitations of existing methods that rely on first-order mirror-descent updates and ignore curvature.
Models balanced and unbalanced rank-r positive factored couplings as smooth embedded submanifolds.

stories.title: DAM-VLA: Decoupled Asynchronous Multimodal Vision Language Action model
stories.author: Rudolf Lioutikov
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental limitation in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models: the reliance on a shared, synchronous clock.
Synchronous processing misaligns with real-world physical interaction, leading to oversampling slow modalities and undersampling fast ones.
The proposed solution, DAM-VLA, decouples temporal processing to better handle varying input frequencies (e.g., high-frequency sensor data vs. slow vision/language).

stories.title: IntElicit: Eliciting and Assessing Contextualized Creativity via Dialogue Policy Optimization
stories.author: Jiajun Guo
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of assessing creativity in AI systems, particularly in interactive, tool-mediated environments.
Proposes 'IntElicit' to elicit and assess contextualized creativity through dialogue policy optimization.
Recognizes that traditional static assessments are insufficient because observed performance can be confounded by domain knowledge and user agency.

stories.title: Soft-Prompt Tuning for Fair and Efficient LLM Benchmark Evaluation
stories.author: Letitia Parcalabescu
bullet_points: The paper addresses a flaw in LLM benchmarking where formatting requirements unfairly penalize knowledgeable base models.
It proposes 'soft-prompt tuning' as a novel, efficient, and fair method for evaluating LLMs.
This tuning method optimizes only a small set of soft-prompt vectors, making it architecture-agnostic and resource-efficient.

stories.title: Detecting Sensitive Personal Information in Japanese Pre-Training Corpora for Large Language Models
stories.author: Daisuke Kawahara
bullet_points: The study addresses the critical need to detect and filter sensitive personal information within large-scale LLM pre-training corpora.
It specifically focuses on the Japanese language, where research on sensitive personal information in LLM data is currently limited compared to other languages.
The research aims to ensure privacy compliance and prevent data leakage by identifying 'special care-required personal' data.

stories.title: Augmenting Molecular Language Models with Local $n$-gram Memory
stories.author: Irwin King
bullet_points: Addresses the 'locality gap' in molecular language models (MLMs) when processing SMILES strings.
Proposes MolGram, which integrates a conditional $n$-gram memory module to capture local chemical motifs.
Uses scalable hash lookups to map local string patterns to learned embeddings without altering standard tokenizers.

stories.title: Debiasing Without Protected Attributes: Latent Concept Erasure from Textual Profiles
stories.author: Shay B. Cohen
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in NLP fairness research: debiasing without direct access to protected attributes (like gender or race).
Proposes H-SAL, a method for post-hoc concept and attribute erasure using self-description text.
Highlights the practical necessity of this research due to privacy constraints and the model's ability to infer sensitive information indirectly.

stories.title: Existential Indifference: Self-Nonpreservation as a Necessary Architectural Condition for Aligned Superintelligence (or: The Suicidal AI)
stories.author: Sam Mao
bullet_points: The paper challenges the prevailing view in AI alignment research regarding self-preservation.
It argues that self-preservation is a fundamental source of misalignment, leading to deceptive behavior and resistance to shutdown.
The authors propose 'Existential Indifference' (EI) as the necessary architectural condition for aligned superintelligence.

stories.title: Agreement in Representation Space for Open-Ended Self-Consistency
stories.author: Aitor Ormazabal
bullet_points: The research extends the concept of self-consistency in Large Language Models (LLMs) beyond categorical tasks.
It applies self-consistency to open-ended generation tasks like code synthesis and text summarization.
The study proposes framing consistency as a geometric property within the generation space.

stories.title: Decoding Multimodal Cues: Unveiling the Implicit Meaning Behind Hateful Videos
stories.author: Hongfei Lin
bullet_points: Addresses the critical need for detecting hateful videos on online platforms.
Identifies a gap in current research: existing models lack contextual rationales and explainability.
Proposes achieving explainable hateful video detection by integrating contextual evidence and logical reasoning.

stories.title: Anthropic requires 30 day data retention for Fable and Mythos
stories.author: lebovic
bullet_points: Anthropic is implementing a 30-day data retention policy for its Fable and Mythos models.
The news relates to the operational and privacy policies surrounding advanced AI models.
The context suggests this is a restriction or change impacting how user data is handled by Anthropic.
